judgment.summary: High-growth semiconductor business with explosive earnings momentum and an attractive PEG ratio, offset by high leverage and a steep trailing valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last reported EPS of 1.37 beat the 1.29 estimate by 5.8%, with the next earnings date scheduled for 2026-08-04.
positives: PEG ratio of 1.02 — suggests attractive valuation relative to growth.
Earnings growth of 91.2% — demonstrates massive bottom-line expansion.
Analyst growth estimate for the current quarter of 234.5% — implies significant upcoming growth potential.
Free cash flow (TTM) of $7.17B — provides a solid cash cushion.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 137.87x — represents a very high valuation relative to current earnings.
Debt/Equity ratio of 6.00 — indicates significant leverage on the balance sheet.
Beta of 2.40 — implies high stock price volatility.
Price/Sales (TTM) of 18.07 — shows a high premium on revenue.

news.paragraph: AMD is seeing positive momentum as Citigroup raised its price target to $460, citing a massive projected $132 billion CPU market. While Citi maintains a neutral rating despite the higher target, other recent reports highlight the company as a strong growth stock. No immediate earnings or regulatory catalysts are mentioned in the current news cycle.
